NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, for the sake of the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus, through the will of God,—according to the promise of life in Christ Jesus;—
text.KJV: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God for the promise of life in Christ Jesus,
text.DRB: Paul an Apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Unto Timothy, my beloved child: Favour, mercy, peace, from God our Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.NABRE: to Timothy, my dear child: grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: to Timothee my dearest son, grace, mercy, peace from God the Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I am grateful to God—whom I worship with a clear conscience, as my ancestors did—when I remember you constantly in my prayers night and day.
text.Rotherham: Grateful, am I, unto God,—unto whom I am rendering divine service from my progenitors in a pure conscience, that, incessant, hold I the remembrance, concerning thee, in my supplications; day and night,
text.KJV: I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;
text.NABRE: Thanksgiving. I am grateful to God, whom I worship with a clear conscience as my ancestors did, as I remember you constantly in my prayers, night and day.
text.DRB: I give thanks to God, whom I serve from my progenitors in a pure conscience, that without intermission I have a memory of thee in my prayers, night and day.

text.NRSV: Recalling your tears, I long to see you so that I may be filled with joy.
text.Rotherham: longing to see thee, being mindful of thy tears,—in order that, with joy, I may be filled:
text.KJV: Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;
text.NABRE: I yearn to see you again, recalling your tears, so that I may be filled with joy,
text.DRB: desiring to see thee, mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy,

text.NRSV: I am reminded of your sincere faith, a faith that lived first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice and now, I am sure, lives in you.
text.Rotherham: A reminder, having received, of the unfeigned faith, that is in thee,—such, as dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and in thy mother Eunice,—I am persuaded, moreover, that [it dwelleth], in thee also.
text.KJV: When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.
text.NABRE: as I recall your sincere faith that first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice and that I am confident lives also in you.
text.DRB: calling to mind that faith which is in thee not feigned, which also dwelt first in thy grand-mother Lois, and thy mother Eunice, and I am sure that in thee also.

text.NRSV: For this reason I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands;
text.Rotherham: For which cause, I put thee in remembrance, to be stirring up God’s gift of favour, which is in thee through means of the laying on of my hands;
text.KJV: Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.
text.NABRE: II. Exhortations to Timothy The Gifts Timothy Has Received. For this reason, I remind you to stir into flame the gift of God that you have through the imposition of my hands.
text.DRB: For the which cause I admonish thee that thou resuscitate the grace of God, which is in thee by the imposition of my hands.

text.NRSV: for God did not give us a spirit of cowardice, but rather a spirit of power and of love and of self-discipline.
text.Rotherham: For God hath not given us a spirit of cowardice, but of power, and love, and correction.
text.KJV: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
text.NABRE: For God did not give us a spirit of cowardice but rather of power and love and self-control.
text.DRB: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear: but of power, and love, and sobriety.

text.NRSV: Do not be ashamed, then, of the testimony about our Lord or of me his prisoner, but join with me in suffering for the gospel, relying on the power of God,
text.Rotherham: Be not thou, therefore, put to shame—with regard to the witness of our Lord, nor with regard to me his prisoner; but suffer hardship together with the glad-message according to the power of God,—
text.KJV: Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;
text.NABRE: So do not be ashamed of your testimony to our Lord, nor of me, a prisoner for his sake; but bear your share of hardship for the gospel with the strength that comes from God.
text.DRB: Be not therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but travail with the Gospel according to the power of God,

text.NRSV: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to his own purpose and grace. This grace was given to us in Christ Jesus before the ages began,
text.Rotherham: Who hath saved us, and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to the peculiar purpose and favour—which was given to us in Christ Jesus before age-during times,
text.KJV: Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,
text.NABRE: He saved us and called us to a holy life, not according to our works but according to his own design and the grace bestowed on us in Christ Jesus before time began,
text.DRB: who hath delivered and called us by his holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before the secular times.

text.NRSV: but it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
text.Rotherham: But hath now been made manifest through means of the forthshining of our Saviour Christ Jesus,—Who, indeed, hath abolished death, and hath thrown light upon life and incorruptibility, through means of the glad-message:
text.KJV: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:
text.NABRE: but now made manifest through the appearance of our savior Christ Jesus, who destroyed death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
text.DRB: But it is manifested now by the illumination of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath destroyed death, and illuminated life and incorruption by the Gospel:

text.NRSV: For this gospel I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher,
text.Rotherham: Whereunto, I, have been appointed herald and apostle and teacher;
text.KJV: Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: for which I was appointed preacher and apostle and teacher.
text.DRB: wherein I am appointed a preacher and Apostle and Master of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: and for this reason I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, for I know the one in whom I have put my trust, and I am sure that he is able to guard until that day what I have entrusted to him.
text.Rotherham: For which cause, these things also, am I suffering; nevertheless I am not being put to shame, for I know him whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is, able, to guard, that which I have entrusted, [to him] unto, that, day.
text.KJV: For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.
text.NABRE: On this account I am suffering these things; but I am not ashamed, for I know him in whom I have believed and am confident that he is able to guard what has been entrusted to me until that day.
text.DRB: For the which cause also I suffer these things: but I am not confounded. For I know whom I have believed, and I am sure that he is able to keep my depositum unto that day.

text.NRSV: Hold to the standard of sound teaching that you have heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: An outline, have thou, of healthful discourses which from me thou hast heard—with the faith and love which are in Christ Jesus:
text.KJV: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: Take as your norm the sound words that you heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: Have thou a form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me in faith and in the love of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Guard the good treasure entrusted to you, with the help of the Holy Spirit living in us.
text.Rotherham: The noble thing entrusted [to thee], do thou guard, through means of the Holy Spirit which dwelleth within us.
text.KJV: That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.
text.NABRE: Guard this rich trust with the help of the holy Spirit that dwells within us.
text.DRB: Keep the good depositum by the Holy Ghost, which dwelleth in us.

text.NRSV: You are aware that all who are in Asia have turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text.Rotherham: Thou knowest this—that all they who are in Asia have turned away from me,—of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text.KJV: This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Suffering. You know that everyone in Asia deserted me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text.DRB: Thou knowest this, that all which are in Asia, be averted from me: of whom is Phigelus and Hermogenes.

text.NRSV: May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain;
text.Rotherham: The Lord grant mercy unto the house, of Onesiphorus, in that, ofttimes, hath he refreshed me, and, as regardeth my chain, hath not been put to shame,
text.KJV: The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:
text.NABRE: May the Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus because he often gave me new heart and was not ashamed of my chains.
text.DRB: Our Lord give mercy to the house of Onesiphorus: because he hath often refreshed me, and hath not been ashamed of my chain:

text.NRSV: when he arrived in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me
text.Rotherham: But, happening to be in Rome, he diligently sought out and found me,—
text.KJV: But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.
text.NABRE: But when he came to Rome, he promptly searched for me and found me.
text.DRB: but when he was come to Rome, he sought me carefully, and found me.

text.NRSV: —may the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well how much service he rendered in Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: The Lord grant him to find mercy from [the] Lord in, that, day;—and, how many times, in Ephesus, he hath ministered, better art, thou, getting to know.
text.KJV: The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.
text.NABRE: May the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day. And you know very well the services he rendered in Ephesus.
text.DRB: Our Lord grant him to find mercy of our Lord in that day. And how many things he ministered to me at Ephesus, thou knowest better.

text.NRSV: You then, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus;
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, my child, be empowering thyself in the favour that is in Christ Jesus,
text.KJV: Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Timothy’s Conduct. So you, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: Thou therefore my son, be strong in the grace which is in Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: and what you have heard from me through many witnesses entrust to faithful people who will be able to teach others as well.
text.Rotherham: And, the things which thou hast heard from me among many witnesses, the same, entrust thou unto faithful men, such as shall be, competent, to teach, others also.
text.KJV: And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.
text.NABRE: And what you heard from me through many witnesses entrust to faithful people who will have the ability to teach others as well.
text.DRB: and the things which thou hast heard of me by many witnesses, these commend to faithful men, which shall be fit to teach others also.

text.NRSV: Share in suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Take thy part in suffering hardship, as a brave soldier of Christ Jesus:—
text.KJV: Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: Bear your share of hardship along with me like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: Labour thou as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: No one serving in the army gets entangled in everyday affairs; the soldier’s aim is to please the enlisting officer.
text.Rotherham: No one that is serving as a soldier, entangleth himself with the matters of his livelihood, that he may please him that hath summoned him to serve as a soldier;
text.KJV: No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.
text.NABRE: To satisfy the one who recruited him, a soldier does not become entangled in the business affairs of life.
text.DRB: No man being a soldier to God, entangleth himself with secular businesses; that he may please him to whom he hath approved himself.

text.NRSV: And in the case of an athlete, no one is crowned without competing according to the rules.
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, any man, contend even in the games, he is not crowned, unless, lawfully, he contend;
text.KJV: And if a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.
text.NABRE: Similarly, an athlete cannot receive the winner’s crown except by competing according to the rules.
text.DRB: For he also that striveth for the mastery, is not crowned unless he strive lawfully.

text.NRSV: It is the farmer who does the work who ought to have the first share of the crops.
text.Rotherham: The toiling husbandman, ought, first, of the fruits, to partake:
text.KJV: The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.
text.NABRE: The hardworking farmer ought to have the first share of the crop.
text.DRB: The husband-man that laboureth, must first take of the fruits.

text.NRSV: Think over what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
text.Rotherham: Think, as to what I am speaking; for the Lord will give thee discernment in all things.
text.KJV: Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
text.NABRE: Reflect on what I am saying, for the Lord will give you understanding in everything.
text.DRB: Understand what I say: for our Lord will give thee in all things understanding.

text.NRSV: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David—that is my gospel,
text.Rotherham: Keep in mind Jesus Christ—raised from among the dead, of the seed of David,—according to my joyful message:
text.KJV: Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:
text.NABRE: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David: such is my gospel,
text.DRB: Be mindful that our Lord Jesus Christ is risen again from the dead, of the seed of David, according to my Gospel,

text.NRSV: for which I suffer hardship, even to the point of being chained like a criminal. But the word of God is not chained.
text.Rotherham: In which I am suffering hardship, even unto bonds, as an evil-doer; but, the word of God, is not bound.
text.KJV: Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.
text.NABRE: for which I am suffering, even to the point of chains, like a criminal. But the word of God is not chained.
text.DRB: wherein I labour even unto bands, as a malefactor: but the word of God is not tied.

text.NRSV: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, so that they may also obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, am I enduring, all things, for the sake of the chosen, in order that, they also, may obtain, the salvation, which is in Christ Jesus along with glory age-abiding.
text.KJV: Therefore I endure all things for the elect’s sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I bear with everything for the sake of those who are chosen, so that they too may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, together with eternal glory.
text.DRB: Therefore I sustain all things for the elect, that they also may obtain the salvation, which is in Christ Jesus, with heavenly glory.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure: If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying—for, If we have died together, we shall also live together,
text.KJV: It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him, we shall also live with him:
text.NABRE: This saying is trustworthy: If we have died with him we shall also live with him;
text.DRB: Afaithful saying. For if we be dead with him, we shall live also together.

text.NRSV: if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he will also deny us;
text.Rotherham: If we endure, we shall also reign together; If we shall deny, he also, will deny us,
text.KJV: If we suffer, we shall also reign with him: if we deny him, he also will deny us:
text.NABRE: if we persevere we shall also reign with him. But if we deny him he will deny us.
text.DRB: If we shall sustain, we shall also reign together. If we shall deny, he also will deny us.

text.NRSV: if we are faithless, he remains faithful— for he cannot deny himself.
text.Rotherham: If we are faithless, he, faithful, abideth,—for, deny himself, he cannot!
text.KJV: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
text.NABRE: If we are unfaithful he remains faithful, for he cannot deny himself.
text.DRB: If we believe not; he continueth faithful, he cannot deny himself.

text.NRSV: Remind them of this, and warn them before God that they are to avoid wrangling over words, which does no good but only ruins those who are listening.
text.Rotherham: Of these things, be putting [them] in remembrance, adjuring [them] before God not to be waging word-battles,—useful, for nothing, occasioning a subversion of them that hearken.
text.KJV: Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.
text.NABRE: III. Instructions Concerning False Teaching Warning Against Useless Disputes. Remind people of these things and charge them before God to stop disputing about words. This serves no useful purpose since it harms those who listen.
text.DRB: These things admonish: testifying before our Lord. Contend not in words, for it is profitable for nothing, but for the subversion of them that hear.

text.NRSV: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved by him, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly explaining the word of truth.
text.Rotherham: Give diligence, thyself, approved, to present unto God,—a workman not to be put to shame, skillfully handling the word of truth.
text.KJV: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
text.NABRE: Be eager to present yourself as acceptable to God, a workman who causes no disgrace, imparting the word of truth without deviation.
text.DRB: Carefully provide to present thyself approved to God, a work-man not to be confounded, rightly handling the word of truth.

text.NRSV: Avoid profane chatter, for it will lead people into more and more impiety,
text.Rotherham: But, the profane pratings, shun; for, unto more ungodliness, will they force themselves on;
text.KJV: But shun profane and vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.
text.NABRE: Avoid profane, idle talk, for such people will become more and more godless,
text.DRB: But profane and vain speeches avoid: for they do much grow to impiety:

text.NRSV: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
text.Rotherham: And, their discourse, as a gangrene, will eat its way;—of whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
text.KJV: And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymeneus and Philetus;
text.NABRE: and their teaching will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
text.DRB: and their speech spreadeth as a canker: of whom is Hymenæus and Philetus:

text.NRSV: who have swerved from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already taken place. They are upsetting the faith of some.
text.Rotherham: Men who, concerning the truth, have erred, affirming, a resurrection, already, to have taken place, and are overthrowing the faith, of some.
text.KJV: Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.
text.NABRE: who have deviated from the truth by saying that [the] resurrection has already taken place and are upsetting the faith of some.
text.DRB: who have erred from the truth, saying that the resurrection is done already, and have subverted the faith of some.

text.NRSV: But God’s firm foundation stands, bearing this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let everyone who calls on the name of the Lord desist from wickedness.”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the firm foundation of God, standeth, having this seal—The Lord hath acknowledged them who are his, and, Let every one that nameth the name of the Lord stand aloof from unrighteousness.
text.KJV: Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.
text.NABRE: Nevertheless, God’s solid foundation stands, bearing this inscription, “The Lord knows those who are his”; and, “Let everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord avoid evil.”
text.DRB: But the sure foundation of God standeth, having this seal, Our Lord knoweth, who be his, and let every one depart from iniquity that nameth the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: In a large house there are utensils not only of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for special use, some for ordinary.
text.Rotherham: But, in a great house, there are not only gold and silver vessels, but, also wooden and earthen: and, some, indeed, for honour, while, some, are for dishonour:
text.KJV: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.
text.NABRE: In a large household there are vessels not only of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for lofty and others for humble use.
text.DRB: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth: and certain indeed unto honour, but certain unto contumely.

text.NRSV: All who cleanse themselves of the things I have mentioned will become special utensils, dedicated and useful to the owner of the house, ready for every good work.
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, anyone will, for pureness, sever himself from these, he shall be a vessel for honour, hallowed, meet for the Master’s use, for every good work, prepared.
text.KJV: If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master’s use, and prepared unto every good work.
text.NABRE: If anyone cleanses himself of these things, he will be a vessel for lofty use, dedicated, beneficial to the master of the house, ready for every good work.
text.DRB: If any man therefore shall cleanse himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified & profitable to our Lord, prepared to every good work.

text.NRSV: Shun youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
text.Rotherham: But, from the youthful covetings, flee! and pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace, along with them who call upon the Lord out of a pure heart.
text.KJV: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
text.NABRE: So turn from youthful desires and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord with purity of heart.
text.DRB: But youthful desires fly, and pursue justice, faith, charity, and peace with them that invocate our Lord from a pure heart.

text.NRSV: Have nothing to do with stupid and senseless controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.
text.Rotherham: But, from the foolish and undisciplined questionings, excuse thyself, knowing that they gender strifes;
text.KJV: But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.
text.NABRE: Avoid foolish and ignorant debates, for you know that they breed quarrels.
text.DRB: And foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they engender brawls.

text.NRSV: And the Lord’s servant must not be quarrelsome but kindly to everyone, an apt teacher, patient,
text.Rotherham: And, a servant of the Lord, ought not to strive, but to be, gentle, towards all, apt in teaching, ready to endure malice,—
text.KJV: And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men, apt to teach, patient,
text.NABRE: A slave of the Lord should not quarrel, but should be gentle with everyone, able to teach, tolerant,
text.DRB: But the servant of our Lord must not wrangle: but be mild toward all men, apt to teach, patient,

text.NRSV: correcting opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant that they will repent and come to know the truth,
text.Rotherham: In meekness, bringing under discipline them that oppose themselves, lest at any time God should give them repentance unto a personal knowledge of truth,
text.KJV: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;
text.NABRE: correcting opponents with kindness. It may be that God will grant them repentance that leads to knowledge of the truth,
text.DRB: with modesty admonishing them that resist the truth: lest sometime God give them repentance to know the truth:

text.NRSV: and that they may escape from the snare of the devil, having been held captive by him to do his will.
text.Rotherham: And they should wake up to sobriety out of, the adversary’s, snare,—though they have been taken alive by him for, that one’s, will.
text.KJV: And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.
text.NABRE: and that they may return to their senses out of the devil’s snare, where they are entrapped by him, for his will.
text.DRB: and they recover themselves from the snares of the devil, of whom they are held captive at his will.

text.NRSV: You must understand this, that in the last days distressing times will come.
text.Rotherham: But, of this, be taking note—that, in last days, there will set in perilous seasons;
text.KJV: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 The Dangers of the Last Days. But understand this: there will be terrifying times in the last days.
text.DRB: And this know thou, that in the last days shall approach perilous times.

text.NRSV: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
text.Rotherham: For men will be—fond of themselves, fond of money, ostentatious, arrogant, defamers, to parents, unyielding, unthankful, unkind,
text.KJV: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
text.NABRE: People will be self-centered and lovers of money, proud, haughty, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, irreligious,
text.DRB: And men shall be lovers of themselves, covetous, haughty, proud, blasphemous, not obedient to their parents, unkind, wicked,

text.NRSV: inhuman, implacable, slanderers, profligates, brutes, haters of good,
text.Rotherham: without natural affection, accepting no truce, given to intrigue, without self-control, uncivilized, unfriendly to good men,
text.KJV: Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,
text.NABRE: callous, implacable, slanderous, licentious, brutal, hating what is good,
text.DRB: without affection, without peace, accusers, incontinent, unmerciful, without benignity,

text.NRSV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
text.Rotherham: traitors, reckless, beclouded, lovers of pleasure more than lovers of God,
text.KJV: Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;
text.NABRE: traitors, reckless, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
text.DRB: traitors, stubborn, puffed up, and lovers of voluptuousness more than of God:

text.NRSV: holding to the outward form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid them!
text.Rotherham: having a form of godliness, but, the power thereof, denying! and, from these, turn away;
text.KJV: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
text.NABRE: as they make a pretense of religion but deny its power. Reject them.
text.DRB: having an appearance indeed of piety, but denying the virtue thereof. And these avoid.

text.NRSV: For among them are those who make their way into households and captivate silly women, overwhelmed by their sins and swayed by all kinds of desires,
text.Rotherham: For, of these, are they who enter into the houses, and captivate silly women, [women] laden with sins, led on by manifold covetings,
text.KJV: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
text.NABRE: For some of these slip into homes and make captives of women weighed down by sins, led by various desires,
text.DRB: For of these be they that craftily enter into houses; & lead captive seely women laden with sins, which are led with diverse desires:

text.NRSV: who are always being instructed and can never arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
text.Rotherham: Ever learning and never, unto a personal knowledge of truth, able to come;
text.KJV: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
text.NABRE: always trying to learn but never able to reach a knowledge of the truth.
text.DRB: always learning, and never attaining to the knowledge of the truth.

text.NRSV: As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people, of corrupt mind and counterfeit faith, also oppose the truth.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, like as, Jannes and Jambres, withstood Moses, so, these men also, withstand the truth,—men utterly corrupted in their mind, disapproved concerning their faith;
text.KJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
text.NABRE: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so they also oppose the truth—people of depraved mind, unqualified in the faith.
text.DRB: But as Iannes and Mambres resisted Moyses, so these also resist the truth, men corrupted in mind, reprobate concerning the faith.

text.NRSV: But they will not make much progress, because, as in the case of those two men, their folly will become plain to everyone.
text.Rotherham: But they shall not force their way further, for, their folly, shall be, fully evident, unto all,—as also, that of those men, became.
text.KJV: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as theirs also was.
text.NABRE: But they will not make further progress, for their foolishness will be plain to all, as it was with those two. Paul’s Example and Teaching.
text.DRB: But they shall prosper no further: for their folly shall be manifest to all, as theirs also was.

text.NRSV: Now you have observed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,
text.Rotherham: But, thou, hast closely studied—my teaching, manner of life, purpose, faith, long-suffering, love, endurance,
text.KJV: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,
text.NABRE: You have followed my teaching, way of life, purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance,
text.DRB: But thou hast attained to my doctrine, institution, purpose, faith, longanimity, love, patience,

text.NRSV: my persecutions, and my suffering the things that happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. What persecutions I endured! Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.
text.Rotherham: persecutions, sufferings, what manner of things, befell me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra, what manner of persecutions, I endured,—and, out of all, the Lord, rescued me;—
text.KJV: Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.
text.NABRE: persecutions, and sufferings, such as happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra, persecutions that I endured. Yet from all these things the Lord delivered me.
text.DRB: persecutions, passions: what manner of things were done to me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra: what manner of persecutions I sustained. And out of all our Lord delivered me.

text.NRSV: Indeed, all who want to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
text.Rotherham: Yea and, all who are determined to live in a godly manner in Christ Jesus, will be persecuted;
text.KJV: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.
text.NABRE: In fact, all who want to live religiously in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
text.DRB: And all that will live godly in Christ Jesus, shall suffer persecution.

text.NRSV: But wicked people and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, wicked men and howling impostors, will force their way to the worse, deceiving and being deceived.
text.KJV: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.
text.NABRE: But wicked people and charlatans will go from bad to worse, deceivers and deceived.
text.DRB: But evil men & seducers shall prosper to the worse: erring, and driving into error.

text.NRSV: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and firmly believed, knowing from whom you learned it,
text.Rotherham: But, thou,—abide in the things which thou hast learned, and been entrusted with, knowing, from whom, thou hast learned [them],—
text.KJV: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
text.NABRE: But you, remain faithful to what you have learned and believed, because you know from whom you learned it,
text.DRB: But thou, continue in those things which thou hast learned, & are committed to thee: knowing of whom thou hast learned;

text.NRSV: and how from childhood you have known the sacred writings that are able to instruct you for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And that, from a babe, those sacred letters, thou hast known which are able to make thee wise unto salvation, through the faith which is in Christ Jesus:
text.KJV: And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: and that from infancy you have known [the] sacred scriptures, which are capable of giving you wisdom for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: & because from thine infancy thou hast known the holy Scriptures, which can instruct thee to salvation, by the faith that is in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: All scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
text.Rotherham: Every scripture, [is] God-breathed, and profitable—unto teaching, unto conviction, unto correction, unto the discipline that is in righteousness,—
text.KJV: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
text.NABRE: All scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for refutation, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
text.DRB: All Scripture inspired of God, is profitable to teach, to argue, to correct, to instruct in justice:

text.NRSV: so that everyone who belongs to God may be proficient, equipped for every good work.
text.Rotherham: In order that, ready, may be the man, of God, unto every good work, being well-prepared.
text.KJV: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.
text.NABRE: so that one who belongs to God may be competent, equipped for every good work.
text.DRB: that the man of God may be perfect, instructed to every good work.

text.NRSV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and in view of his appearing and his kingdom, I solemnly urge you:
text.Rotherham: I adjure [thee] before God, and Christ Jesus—Who is about to be judging living and dead,—both as to his forthshining and his kingdom,
text.KJV: I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Solemn Charge. I charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who will judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingly power:
text.DRB: I Testify before God and Jesus Christ who shall judge the living and the dead, and by his advent, and his Kingdom:

text.NRSV: proclaim the message; be persistent whether the time is favorable or unfavorable; convince, rebuke, and encourage, with the utmost patience in teaching.
text.Rotherham: Proclaim the word, take thy position—in season, out of season,—convince, rebuke, encourage,—with all long-suffering and teaching.
text.KJV: Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.
text.NABRE: proclaim the word; be persistent whether it is convenient or inconvenient; convince, reprimand, encourage through all patience and teaching.
text.DRB: Preach the word. Urge in season, out of season, reprove, beseech, rebuke, in all patience and doctrine.

text.NRSV: For the time is coming when people will not put up with sound doctrine, but having itching ears, they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own desires,
text.Rotherham: For there will be a season—when, the healthful teaching, they will not endure, but, according to their own covetings, will, unto themselves, heap up teachers, because they have an itching ear,
text.KJV: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;
text.NABRE: For the time will come when people will not tolerate sound doctrine but, following their own desires and insatiable curiosity, will accumulate teachers
text.DRB: For there shall be a time when they will not bear sound doctrine: but according to their own desires they will heap to themselves masters, having itching ears,

text.NRSV: and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander away into myths.
text.Rotherham: And, from the truth, indeed, their ear, will they turn away, while, unto stories, they will turn themselves aside.
text.KJV: And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.
text.NABRE: and will stop listening to the truth and will be diverted to myths.
text.DRB: and from the truth certes they will avert their hearing, and to fables they will be converted.

text.NRSV: As for you, always be sober, endure suffering, do the work of an evangelist, carry out your ministry fully.
text.Rotherham: But, thou,—be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do, the work, of an evangelist, thy ministry, completely fulfill;
text.KJV: But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.
text.NABRE: But you, be self-possessed in all circumstances; put up with hardship; perform the work of an evangelist; fulfill your ministry.
text.DRB: But be thou vigilant, labour in all things, do the work of an Evangelist, fulfill thy ministry. Be sober.

text.NRSV: As for me, I am already being poured out as a libation, and the time of my departure has come.
text.Rotherham: For, I, already, am being poured out as a drink-offering, and, the season of my release, is at hand,—
text.KJV: For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.
text.NABRE: Reward for Fidelity. For I am already being poured out like a libation, and the time of my departure is at hand.
text.DRB: For I am even now to be sacrificed: and the time of my resolution is at hand.

text.NRSV: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
text.Rotherham: The noble contest, have I contested, the race, have I finished, the faith, have I kept:
text.KJV: I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:
text.NABRE: I have competed well; I have finished the race; I have kept the faith.
text.DRB: I have fought a good fight, I have consummate my course, I have kept the faith.

text.NRSV: From now on there is reserved for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have longed for his appearing.
text.Rotherham: Henceforth, lieth by for me—the crown, of righteousness, which the Lord will render unto me in that, day,—The righteous judge,—Ye, not alone unto me, but unto all them also who have loved his forthshining.
text.KJV: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
text.NABRE: From now on the crown of righteousness awaits me, which the Lord, the just judge, will award to me on that day, and not only to me, but to all who have longed for his appearance.
text.DRB: Concerning the rest, there is laid up for me a crown of justice, which our Lord will render to me in that day, a just judge: and not only to me, but to them also that love his coming.

text.NRSV: Do your best to come to me soon,
text.Rotherham: Give diligence to come unto me speedily,
text.KJV: Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:
text.NABRE: IV. Personal Requests and Final Greetings Paul’s Loneliness. Try to join me soon,
text.DRB: Make haste to come to me quickly.

text.NRSV: for Demas, in love with this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
text.Rotherham: For, Demas, hath forsaken me, having loved the present age, and hath journeyed unto Thessalonica; Crescens unto Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia:
text.KJV: For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.
text.NABRE: for Demas, enamored of the present world, deserted me and went to Thessalonica, Crescens to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.
text.DRB: For Demas hath left me, loving this world, and is gone to Thessalonica: Crescens into Galatia, Titus into Dalmatia.

text.NRSV: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful in my ministry.
text.Rotherham: Luke, alone is with me. Receiving, Mark, back, bring him with thyself, for he is very useful to me for ministering;
text.KJV: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.
text.NABRE: Luke is the only one with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is helpful to me in the ministry.
text.DRB: Luke only is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.

text.NRSV: I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: and, Tychicus, have I sent unto Ephesus.
text.KJV: And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
text.NABRE: I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text.DRB: But Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.

text.NRSV: When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, also the books, and above all the parchments.
text.Rotherham: The cloak that I left in Troas, with Carpus, when thou comest, bring; and the scrolls, especially, the parchments.
text.KJV: The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments.
text.NABRE: When you come, bring the cloak I left with Carpus in Troas, the papyrus rolls, and especially the parchments.
text.DRB: The cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus, coming bring with thee, and the books, especially the parchment.

text.NRSV: Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will pay him back for his deeds.
text.Rotherham: Alexander the coppersmith, of much baseness towards me, hath given proof,—the Lord will render unto him according to his works.—
text.KJV: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:
text.NABRE: Alexander the coppersmith did me a great deal of harm; the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
text.DRB: Alexander the Coppersmith hath shewed me much evil: our Lord will reward him according to his works:

text.NRSV: You also must beware of him, for he strongly opposed our message.
text.Rotherham: Of whom, be, thou also, on thy guard, for he hath greatly withstood our words.
text.KJV: Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.
text.NABRE: You too be on guard against him, for he has strongly resisted our preaching.
text.DRB: whom do thou also avoid, for he hath greatly resisted our words.

text.NRSV: At my first defense no one came to my support, but all deserted me. May it not be counted against them!
text.Rotherham: In my first defence, no man, came in to help me, but, all, forsook me,—unto them, may it not be reckoned!—
text.KJV: At my first answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their charge.
text.NABRE: At my first defense no one appeared on my behalf, but everyone deserted me. May it not be held against them!
text.DRB: In my first answer no man was with me, but all did forsake me: be it not imputed to them.

text.NRSV: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it. So I was rescued from the lion’s mouth.
text.Rotherham: But, the Lord, stood by me, and empowered me, in order that, through me, the proclamation, might be fully made, and, all the nations, might hear; and I was delivered out of the mouth of a lion:—
text.KJV: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
text.NABRE: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that through me the proclamation might be completed and all the Gentiles might hear it. And I was rescued from the lion’s mouth.
text.DRB: But our Lord stood to me, and strengthened me, that by me the preaching may be accomplished, and all Gentiles may hear: and I was delivered from the mouth of the lion.

text.NRSV: The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack and save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: The Lord will rescue me from every wicked work, and will bring me safe into his heavenly kingdom: unto whom be the glory, unto the ages of ages. Amen.
text.KJV: And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: The Lord will rescue me from every evil threat and will bring me safe to his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath delivered me from all evil work: and will save me unto his heavenly Kingdom. To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

text.NRSV: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
text.Rotherham: Salute Prisca and Aquila and the house of Onesiphorus.
text.KJV: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
text.NABRE: Final Greeting. Greet Prisca and Aquila and the family of Onesiphorus.
text.DRB: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.

text.NRSV: Erastus remained in Corinth; Trophimus I left ill in Miletus.
text.Rotherham: Erastus, remained in Corinth; but, Trophimus, I left at Miletus, sick.
text.KJV: Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.
text.NABRE: Erastus remained in Corinth, while I left Trophimus sick at Miletus.
text.DRB: Erastus remained at Corinth. And Trophimus I left sick at Miletum.

text.NRSV: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers and sisters.
text.Rotherham: Give diligence to come, before winter. There salute thee—Eubulus, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and [all] the brethren.
text.KJV: Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.
text.NABRE: Try to get here before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers send greetings.
text.DRB: Make haste to come before winter. Eubulus and Pudens and Linus and Claudia, and all the brethren, salute thee.

text.NRSV: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
text.Rotherham: The Lord, be with thy spirit. Favour, be with you.
text.KJV: The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen. The second epistle unto Timotheus, ordained the first bishop of the church of the Ephesians, was written from Rome, when Paul was brought before Nero the second time.
text.NABRE: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with all of you.
text.DRB: Our Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
